name: @forgeax/parity-instancing-static
description: M4 fixture for AC-09: 10000 static cubes (20*20*25 grid) drawn as a single instanced draw call. Locked parameters mirror requirements.md AC-09 (camera, light, canvas, clear color). The AC-09 P95>=60 fps gate is currently disabled (bug-20260612 — fps was fake-green on swiftshader-WebGL2 because the renderer was throwing); re-enable when a hardware-WebGPU CI runner is wired.

bundle-size.reason: vite app bundle (dist/assets) shape is downstream of @forgeax/engine-runtime + @forgeax/engine-rhi-webgpu sizes already tracked at the package level

fps.reason: bug-20260612: AC-09 P95>=60 fps gate had been silently fake-green — the renderer was throwing on createBindGroup in the wgpu-wasm Channel 3 fallback path (no GPU adapter on GH-hosted runners → headless chromium fell back to swiftshader WebGL2), and rAF kept ticking at vsync without rendering, so run-fps measured 60.06 fps every time. With the fallback chain repaired, 10000 cubes actually render on swiftshader, but the swiftshader software path slows progressively (sample1 ~28 fps, sample2 ~11 fps, sample3+ stalls past CI budget) — likely bind-group / buffer accumulation that's invisible on real hardware. AC-09 P95>=60 fps cannot be validated on the GH-hosted ubuntu metrics-validate runner; needs a hardware-WebGPU runner (chrome-beta + lavapipe + --enable-unsafe-swiftshader, or a self-hosted GPU runner). Tracked separately.

bench.reason: fps kind covers steady-state perf for this fixture; pixel-parity bench focus stays on apps/parity/forgeax vs apps/parity/threejs

gate.reason: no package-level binary gate; AC-09 P95>=60 fps real-perf gate needs a hardware-WebGPU runner (the fps kind above is currently disabled — see its `reason`)

spike-report.reason: not a spike app; instanced-mesh design discussion lives in plan-strategy.md, not in this workspace
